behaviors.description: Error message names missing required key by exact config path (e.g., 'database.host')
behaviors.justification: The failure is purely algorithmic: validate required keys and format an error message. No I/O boundary—mock the file read and pass test data (YAML dict missing a key). The test verifies the error message includes the path. Mocks won't miss this because we're testing logic, not filesystem behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Mock config file I/O to return YAML missing a required key (e.g., database.host absent). Call load_config() and assert the ConfigError exception message contains the exact key path 'database.host'.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test directly tests the validation and error-message-formatting logic without filesystem.

behaviors.description: XDG_CONFIG_HOME environment variable redirects config file path resolution
behaviors.justification: Path resolution can have subtle OS-specific bugs (e.g., trailing slashes, symlink handling, double slashes). A Unit test mocking file I/O can verify the right path string is passed to open(), but it won't catch path-joining bugs that only fail with a real filesystem. Integration testing with temp directories catches these edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary directory tree; set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a custom temp path; call load_config() without pre-creating the config file; assert the loader successfully reads from <XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/tool/config.yaml. Verify config values match expected defaults.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test ensures path resolution works correctly on the actual filesystem, not just in mocked assertions.

behaviors.description: TOOL_* environment variables override config file values when both present
behaviors.justification: The failure is a logic bug in the merge step: environment variables should shadow file values. This is pure algorithmic merging, with no I/O boundary. Mock os.environ and file I/O separately; verify the final config object uses the environment variable value. Mocks won't miss this because the merge logic is deterministic and testable in isolation.
behaviors.test_description: Mock os.environ to set TOOL_DATABASE_HOST='env-override' and mock config file containing 'database.host: file-value'. Call load_config(); assert config.database.host == 'env-override'.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test verifies the merge precedence logic without filesystem or subprocess.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax error message includes line number where error was detected
behaviors.justification: The failure is in error-message formatting, not file I/O. The YAML parser provides line-number context; the code must extract and include it in the error message. Mock file I/O to return invalid YAML; the parser will raise an error with line info. Verify the error message includes the line number. Mocks won't miss this because we're testing exception handling and message formatting.
behaviors.test_description: Mock config file to return invalid YAML (e.g., malformed syntax on line 3). Call load_config(); assert the ConfigError message includes line number 3 (or nearby).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test verifies error formatting by mocking the file and validating exception output.

behaviors.description: Config directory and defaults file are created on first run if missing
behaviors.justification: Creating directories and writing files are OS operations with failure modes that mocks hide: permission denied, race conditions (concurrent calls), symlink issues, missing parent directories. Mocking Path.mkdir() and Path.write_text() will always succeed even if the real operations would fail. Testing with a real temp filesystem catches these OS-level failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp directory; set HOME or XDG_CONFIG_HOME to temp; ensure config directory does not exist. Call load_config(). Assert that ~/.config/tool/ (or XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tool) directory exists and contains a config.yaml file with valid default YAML content.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test verifies directory creation and file writing against a real temp filesystem, catching permission and symlink failures that mocks would hide.
